steps.title: Identify the charity with the greatest percent of expenses on programs
steps.content: Scan the scatterplot for the highest point on the y-axis. The topmost data point lies at approximately 4,000 million dollars (x-axis) with an actual percent of about 92% spent on programs (y-axis). This is the charity we need to analyze.

steps.title: Read the predicted value from the line of best fit
steps.content: At x = 4,000 on the line of best fit, trace vertically up to the line and read the corresponding y-value. The line passes through this x-coordinate at approximately 85% on the y-axis. This is the predicted percent according to the regression model.

steps.title: Calculate the residual (actual minus predicted)
steps.content:
The residual is the difference between the actual data point and the line's prediction:

$$\text{Difference} = 92 - 85 = 7\%$$

The actual percent (92%) lies 7 percentage points above the predicted value (85%). That matches choice B — B is correct.
